title: Orbiting ring of something
wikitext:
[]: Orbiting ring of something             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Jul 29, 2013 1:15 am The ring around the Earth at the end of EoE is souls, right? I'm almost certain it is, but some people have been saying it's LCL and I need to know exactly which is right.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 29, 2013 1:19 am Could you provide a screenshot to let us know exactly what you're talking about? I think you might be referring to the bloodstreak that spouted from Giant Naked Rei's neck and into space, staining the moon. The blood that missed the moon continued passed it, but is still visible from Earth at the time. If that is indeed what you're referring to, then yeah it's just blood.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Jul 29, 2013 1:31 am [URL=http://s1041.photobucket.com/user/Kazuo_takada/media/vlcsnap-2013-07-29-02h20m38s163_zps2d539210.png.html] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 29, 2013 1:34 am I've always taken "red stuff out of GNR" to be souls, myself.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 29, 2013 2:09 am Huh, I never realized that the "blood" actually does form a bit of a ring around Earth. That could just be arch GNR made as she was bleeding. Though gravity keeping the "blood" within orbit is a good explanation, it wouldn't consider how a good portion of it stained the moon and even went beyond it. Perhaps a bit of both? Actually, now that I think about it, the black moon did explode in blood/souls which could be the source of the ring, while the blood on the moon in fact belongs to GNR only.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Jul 29, 2013 10:10 am Never noticed that. Sharp eye, Username. The red ring seems to exist more because of aesthethic impact than any in-universe logic and doesn't seem to make physical sense, but between blood or souls i would think it's souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Mon Jul 29, 2013 11:56 am The liquid from the black moon, which becomes the oceans, is the souls of all life in the form of LCL. The liquid on the moon and orbiting the earth is the blood of Lilith, which is also LCL, but no, it's not souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 29, 2013 1:52 pm I'm surprised that the orbital megastructure has gone unremarked by so many. It was apparent even in the Manga Ent Vaseline-o-vision encoding. Perhaps, like other things in that sequence, it depends what you're prepared for in the context.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 29, 2013 2:08 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I'm surprised that the orbital megastructure has gone unremarked by so many. It was apparent even in the Manga Ent Vaseline-o-vision encoding. Perhaps, like other things in that sequence, it depends what you're prepared for in the context.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jul 29, 2013 2:46 pm I always assumed the ring was souls; the souls returning to earth from the heavens makes thematic sense.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Aug 02, 2013 3:35 pm My default assumption is that the ring is blood/LCL left over from the spray that came out of GNR/GNL's neck as it died.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Aug 05, 2013 5:53 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: My default assumption is that the ring is blood/LCL left over from the spray that came out of GNR/GNL's neck as it died.

[]:             Quote Post by Melchior » Mon Aug 05, 2013 8:29 pm Another point to bring up would be the fact that if it's blood or LCL, it's probably a frozen solid ring... On the off chance it's souls... well, I don't know the freezing point for them... If they are frozen, it would likely keep itself equidistant from Earth and never return due to the equal gravitational force applied throughout. Doesn't particularly contribute to "why it's there", but perhaps more of a "why it's not going anywhere?"...
